findings.summary: POST /v1/photos/connectors/test and POST /v1/photos/connectors with empty body return 502 BAD_GATEWAY for what is a local input-validation failure ('immich: base_url is required'). Should map to 400 INVALID_REQUEST since the request never reached an upstream.
findings.closureCommit: harden-040-2026-05-07 (resolve via: git log --grep='close chaos C-001..C-006')

findings.summary: POST /v1/photos/actions/plan with scope.photo_ids:['not-a-uuid'] returns 200 and mints a real action token (photo_count:1). UUID format validation happens later at confirm time. No data harm (confirm rejects), but the plan endpoint should validate UUID format up front.
findings.closureCommit: harden-040-2026-05-07 (resolve via: git log --grep='close chaos C-001..C-006')

findings.summary: GET /v1/photos/connectors advertises both immich AND photoprism, but POST /v1/photos/connectors[/test] rejects provider='photoprism' with 'only immich photo connectors are supported in this scope'. Either drop PhotoPrism from the list endpoint or allow the connect path to accept it.
findings.closureCommit: harden-040-2026-05-07 (resolve via: git log --grep='close chaos C-001..C-006')
findings.resolutionPath: WIRE-UP — register the existing photoprism adapter (NewClient + Connect/Sync/Capabilities/EnumerateScope) on the connect path via a provider dispatcher (photoLibraryFromRequest) so the list endpoint and connect endpoint agree. Adapter was already complete and feature-flagged into ListConnectors; only the connect path needed the dispatcher.

findings.summary: GET /v1/photos/health/duplicates/{id} (404) and POST /v1/photos/health/duplicates/{id}/best-pick (400) leak the raw lib/pq sentinel 'no rows in result set' to the client. Scrub internal error wording before reaching the response.
findings.closureCommit: harden-040-2026-05-07 (resolve via: git log --grep='close chaos C-001..C-006')

findings.summary: GET /v1/photos/search?q=<control-chars> returns 500 photo_search_failed. Other malformed queries (mega body, negative limit, emoji) return clean 200. Validate/strip control characters at handler. Same finding family as 038 G3 — fold into one hardening pass.
findings.closureCommit: harden-040-2026-05-07 (resolve via: git log --grep='close chaos C-001..C-006')

findings.summary: POST /v1/photos/actions/plan accepts scope with 200 random UUIDs (photo_count:200) and mints token without an upper bound. Worth bounding scope size at plan time to prevent very-large-token resource amplification.
findings.closureCommit: harden-040-2026-05-07 (resolve via: git log --grep='close chaos C-001..C-006')

What CAN be defensively closed in this iteration: tighten the MintReveal surface by REMOVING the body-source for `actor_id` (one of the two attack surfaces the audit identified), require it from a header (X-Actor-Id) only, and add production-mode strictness on actor presence. This removes the body-smuggling attack vector entirely while preserving the documented single-tenant trust boundary semantics for dev/test ergonomics. Code changes: (1) internal/api/photos_upload.go — REMOVED the `ActorID string` field from `PhotoRevealRequest` struct (24-byte change at the type level); rewrote `MintReveal` to read the request body once into bytes, mechanically reject any body that contains the literal `"actor_id"` JSON key with HTTP 400 `actor_id_in_body_forbidden` (message points the caller at the X-Actor-Id header), then JSON-unmarshal the now-cleansed bytes into the slimmer `PhotoRevealRequest{TTLSeconds int}`; resolves actor identity ONLY from `r.Header.Get("X-Actor-Id")` (NO fallback to body); when `h.environment == "production"` AND the header is empty, rejects with HTTP 400 `actor_id_required` (message: 'X-Actor-Id header is required in production'); when the header is empty in dev/test, falls back to the literal 'system' actor (preserves existing dev ergonomic). Reordered the store-nil short-circuit to fire AFTER the validation chain so the unit-test surface can exercise validation without a real DB. Added `bytes` import. (2) internal/api/photos.go — added `environment string` field to `PhotosHandlers` struct (with comment block explaining MIT-040-S-003 contract); updated `NewPhotosHandlers(store, cfg, environment string)` signature to accept the environment string and store it. (3) cmd/core/wiring.go — buildAPIDeps now passes `cfg.Environment` (already plumbed end-to-end by MIT-040-S-004 at commit 01ace60) into `NewPhotosHandlers`. (4) tests/integration/photos_health_test.go + tests/integration/photos_capability_taxonomy_canary_test.go + tests/integration/photos_chaos_closure_test.go — updated 3 `api.NewPhotosHandlers(store, cfg)` call sites to `api.NewPhotosHandlers(store, cfg, "test")` to match the new signature. NO HTTP/integration test sites were sending `actor_id` in the request body before this change (verified by `grep -rn '"actor_id"' --include='*.go' --include='*.html' --include='*.js' --include='*.yml' --include='*.yaml' --include='*.py' .` returning ZERO results across the entire codebase; the only HTTP reveal call site is tests/e2e/photos_sensitivity_retrieval_test.go:79 which already sends `{"ttl_seconds": 30}` with no actor_id), so no test migrations were required. The `ActorID:` references in tests/integration/photos_sensitivity_security_test.go (5 sites) and tests/integration/photos_sensitivity_test.go (2 sites) refer to the Go struct field `photolib.MintRevealTokenInput.ActorID` used directly via `store.MintRevealToken(...)` at the storage layer — they are NOT JSON body fields and are correctly preserved. Adversarial regression tests (4 new tests in NEW file internal/api/mint_reveal_actor_id_s003_test.go, ~189 lines): TestMintReveal_S003_BodySourceForActorIDIsRejected — POSTs reveal request with body `{"actor_id":"alice"}` + header `X-Actor-Id: header-actor`, asserts HTTP 400 with code `actor_id_in_body_forbidden`; FAILS if `ActorID` field is reintroduced on `PhotoRevealRequest` OR the `bytes.Contains(bodyBytes, []byte("\"actor_id\""))` smuggling guard is removed; TestMintReveal_S003_HeaderSourcedActorIDIsAccepted — POSTs reveal request with empty body `{}` + header `X-Actor-Id: alice`, asserts the validation chain passes (no 400 `actor_id_in_body_forbidden`, no 400 `actor_id_required`) and the request reaches the store-nil short-circuit returning HTTP 503 `photos_store_unavailable`; FAILS if the header-source path is broken (would be rejected at validation with a 4xx); TestMintReveal_S003_ProductionEnvRequiresActorHeader — constructs `&PhotosHandlers{environment:"production"}`, POSTs request with NO `X-Actor-Id` header, asserts HTTP 400 with code `actor_id_required` (message naming the header); FAILS if the production-mode strictness gate is removed or `h.environment == "production"` check is widened; TestMintReveal_S003_DevelopmentEnvAllowsMissingActorHeader — sub-tests for environment=development AND environment=test, each POSTs request with NO `X-Actor-Id` header, asserts the validation chain does NOT trip the `actor_id_required` gate and the request reaches the store-nil short-circuit (HTTP 503); FAILS if the production-mode gate fires for non-production environments.
